VIX Futures - Individual Market Analysis
← Back to dashboard

VIX Futures - Individual Market Analysis

CoT signals and diagnostics for VIX futures.

As of 2026-02-17

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_Net_%OI of -19.2%, a Spec_zscore of -0.737, and positive 4-week flow, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal is currently in a Normal regime with a Balanced market structure, indicating no clear dominance between speculators and hedgers. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Positioning stretch (Spec_zscore) uses a |z| < 1 threshold to identify un-stretched, normal market conditions. (1)
  • Flow dynamics (Flow_4w) evaluate conviction; flow opposing net direction signifies de-risking or early turn risk. (1)
  • VIX-specific rules interpret speculative positioning as demand for convexity and crash protection rather than a directional equity view. (1)
  • Data Freshness: Stale (Latest observation: 2026-02-17).

Key Dynamics:

  • Speculative net short positioning (-19.2%) lacks intensity, as the Spec_zscore (-0.73) remains within the neutral 1-standard-deviation band. (1)
  • Conviction is fading as 4-week flow (+3.2) opposes the net short direction, suggesting early de-risking by speculators. (1)
  • The controller gap is negligible (-0.3%), supporting a balanced market structure without crowding. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A move in Spec_zscore beyond |z| ≥ 1, indicating a transition into a stretched regime. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Mean reversion or range-bound activity as positioning remains un-stretched and balanced.
  • Most plausible upside risk: Short-covering rally in VIX futures if positive flow momentum accelerates toward a squeeze risk regime.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Resumption of the short trend if speculative flow turns negative and reinforces the net short bias.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), based on the responsiveness of 4-week flow and weekly CoT reporting.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, due to the lack of extreme positioning stretch and stale data classification.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs systemic sentiment and demand for crash protection (convexity).
  • Cycle position: Mid-cycle/Neutral, characterized by the absence of systemic fragility or crowding.
  • Typically acts as a counter-cyclical gauge when combined with equity index positioning signals.

Data & References:

  • VIX Futures CoT Diagnostics (Latest: 2026-02-17). (1)
  • Speculative Net % of Open Interest and Spec_zscore are the primary drivers of the current Normal regime. (1)
  • Integration of front-month VIX futures basis and S&P 500 realized volatility would improve depth.
